titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Proof of the Pudding, Yale University, Records
Proof of the Pudding, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 15, 2020 at 07:02

did.unittitle: Proof of the Pudding, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of performance and event ephemera, scrapbooks, photographs and musical recordings from Proof of the Pudding, the second women's a cappella group at Yale University.

bioghist.head: Organizational History
bioghist.p: Proof of the Pudding is Yale's second-oldest all-female undergraduate a cappella group. Adrianne Benton Furniss '76 and Virginia Welles '76 established the group in 1975, a mere six years after women were first admitted to Yale. The name "Proof of the Pudding" is taken from Miguel de Cervantes' Don Quixote: "The proof of the pudding is in the tasting." Given the male-dominated world of Yale in 1975, the founding members of "Proof" had to prove themselves as veritable musicians. The name urged people not to dismiss Proof before they heard them sing. Their deep commitment and musicality was a recipe for instant success, setting the bar high for the next 40 years.

Proof has its roots in jazz and swing, but has more recently expanded their repertoire to include a variety of music. Since its beginning, Proof has been known for its vibrant sound, enthusiastic performance style, and energetic repertoire. It has toured both domestically (most recently in Birmingham, Portland, Boston, and Chicago) and internationally (including Costa Rica, France, and India). Proof has also recorded a number of albums, releasing a new album every three to four years. Recent releases include Paint the Town (2010) and Whisper Low (2014). Our latest album, Beyond the Sea, was released in January 2017 - give it a listen on our Music page!

odd.head: General
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 40 (YRG 40), Records of Yale clubs, societies, and organizations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Proof of the Pudding, Yale University, Records (RU 1170). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of performance and event ephemera, scrapbooks, photographs and musical recordings from Proof of the Pudding, the second women's a cappella group at Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by accession.

did.unittitle: Rush and tap audition ephemera and registration forms

odd.head: General
odd.p: Identified indivduals and events are on the verso.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Identified individuals and events are on the verso.

did.unittitle: Far From the Home I Love
High School chorus sheet music-Alexandra Shade, YC '15
I Only Have Eyes For You
I'm Beginning to See The Light
Look at Me, I'm Sandra Dee
Lord Blow The Moon Out Please
Mama Said (There'd Be Days Like This)
On A Slow Boat to China
Sister's Are Doin' It For Themselves
They Can't Take That Away From Me
